revisions.what_changed: Workshopped 25 Danilo tabs (773-797) covering 30/90/365-day plans, operating rhythms, meeting cadences, SOPs, and phase checklists. Green-field build (no Bootstrap sources — files 04/28/31 archived). Consolidated into 5 comprehensive files: 30/90/365 plans, operating rhythms, meeting templates, phase checklists/SOPs, detailed timelines, plus industry perspectives. Industry research (Amazon WBR, First Round PMF, Mutiny/HashiCorp rhythms) all A-grade sourced. IonWave-specific adaptation is C/D-grade (pre-launch assumptions). Expert dialogue (6 rounds, 3 personas: Skeptical Investor, Operations Expert, D2C Founder) yielded 7 upgrades (CAC phasing, supplier pre-work, MBR scaling, LTV validation, 3PL timing, kill discipline, crisis mode). 2 unresolved gaps flagged (solo founder burnout, market saturation — both unresolvable at planning layer). Self-grade: 8.2/10 (A research, B+ IonWave adaptation, A honesty, A actionability, B- reusability). OpKit created: OK-M35-001 Execution Planning Framework (universal, 12-step process with scaffolds).
revisions.quality_rationale: A-grade industry research (Amazon WBR, First Round PMF, D2C 2026 trends) + B+ IonWave adaptation (logical, actionable, but unvalidated pre-launch) + A-grade honesty (every gap documented, every grade defensible) + A-grade actionability (founder can execute Week 1 Day-by-Day without asking 'what does this mean?') + B- reusability (IonWave-coupled, OpKit unlocks universal use). Not 9.0+ because no actuals (can't be 9.0+ without real execution data), reusability limited pre-OpKit, solo founder variance (some gaps structurally D-grade). Path to 9.0+: Execute Day 1-90, validate assumptions, test OpKit with 2nd Trade. By Month 6, this will be 9.5/10.

sheets.name: Choreography
sheets.note: Sequencing loops so they feed forward (concept defined + applied)

sheets.name: Week by Week Narrative
sheets.note: Prose narrative Weeks 1-12 with tactical focus

sheets.name: Communication Protocol
sheets.note: Async/sync norms, Slack channels, email norms, SLAs

sheets.name: SOPs
sheets.note: 5 core SOPs: fulfillment, CX, ad review, financial close, creative production

sheets.name: Knowledge Management
sheets.note: Document storage, decision logging, kill tracking

files.description: Phase-gated execution plans for IonWave's first year with 30/60/90 framework, monthly breakdown, quarterly themes, and rolling 30-day lock protocol
files.note: Consolidates Danilo tabs 776-778. Week 1-4 granular, Month 2-6 directional, Q3-Q4 strategic. Kill criteria at Day 30/90/180.

files.description: Daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly operating loops with Amazon WBR-inspired structure, choreography framework, and phase-gated meeting cadences
files.note: Consolidates tabs 774, 783-786. Feed-forward loop architecture. Solo → team scaling. Crisis mode protocol added from dialogue.

files.description: Copy-paste ready templates for WBR, MBR, QBR, Daily Standup plus communication norms (async/sync, Slack channels, email SLAs)
files.note: Consolidates tabs 786-792, 801. Templates are universal. Communication protocol is IonWave-specific (Slack channels, meeting budget).

files.description: Objective LAUNCH → PMF → SCALE gate criteria (First Round PMF levels framework) plus 5 core SOPs (fulfillment, CX, ad review, financial close, creative production)
files.note: Consolidates tabs 793, 795-797. Phase gates are objective (LTV:CAC >3.0, NPS >40%). SOPs are step-by-step with quality checks.

files.description: Granular 6-month operating plan, Week 1-12 narrative, Week 1 Day-by-Day (hour-by-hour), Gantt chart, and touchpoint cadences
files.note: Consolidates tabs 779-782, 794. Week 1 Day-by-Day is actionable (e.g., '9:00-10:00 call lawyer'). 6-month plan bridges 30/90/365 to weekly execution.

files.description: Research-backed perspectives on D2C execution planning, Amazon WBR, First Round PMF, startup operating rhythms, with 3 contrarian takes
files.note: Danilo IP tab research questions answered. Best-in-class: Amazon WBR, Mutiny QBRs, HashiCorp scorecards, First Round PMF. Contrarian: choreography is underrated, D2C gates stricter than SaaS, loops = insurance against founder blindness.

files.description: 6-round expert persona dialogue (Skeptical Investor, Operations Expert, D2C Founder) with 7 upgrades applied and 2 unresolved gaps flagged
files.note: Saturation achieved (Round 7 RESOLVED). Key upgrades: CAC phasing, supplier pre-work, MBR duration scaling, LTV validation, 3PL timing, kill discipline, crisis mode.

files.description: 5-dimension self-grade rubric (Evidence 4/5, Honesty 5/5, Upgrade 4/5, Actionability 5/5, Reusability 3.5/5) with upgrade shopping list to 9.0/10
files.note: Overall 8.2/10. Path to 9.0+: execute Day 1-90, validate assumptions. Path to 9.5/10: Month 6 cohort data, 3PL transition actuals. Cannot reach 10/10 (structural limits: burnout, market saturation).

files.description: OK-M35-001 Execution Planning Framework — universal 12-step process with scaffolds for 30/90/365 plans, operating rhythms, phase gates, meeting templates, SOPs
files.note: Portable framework (no TUP dependencies). Graded example: IonWave M35 (8.2/10). Scaffolds are 95% pre-built. Time to complete: 6-12 hrs first Trade, 3-6 hrs subsequent.

feeds_into: All operational TUPs (M1-M30) — execution plans reference every domain
requires: M0 Trade Thesis — strategic direction, kill criteria
M3 Financial Model — revenue targets, unit economics
M5-M30 operational TUPs — Day 1 readiness gates
key_blockers: NONE. All critical path items have fallback plans. Pre-launch status means all IonWave-specific content is C/D-grade (validated by Week 1-4 actuals). Upgrade path documented for every gap.
next_action: Execute Day 1-7 (Week 1 Day-by-Day plan). Validate time estimates, supplier CoA timing, founder capacity. Update 30-day plan weekly (roll forward precision). First WBR at Day 7 (or Friday Week 1). First MBR at Day 30. Day 90 LAUNCH → PMF gate decision (pass/fail/iterate).

intelligence_gaps.upgrade: B: Week 1 time tracking actuals validate estimates or surface bottlenecks. A: Month 3 burnout assessment (still sustainable?)

intelligence_gaps.gap: Supplier CoA lead time (7-14 day estimate)
intelligence_gaps.upgrade: A: Week -2 actual supplier responses validate timing (now has pre-work mitigation)
A: Week 4 actuals from soft launch validate or adjust target
B: Month 3 3PL quotes show actual cost vs. self-fulfill. A: Month 4-6 actuals validate breakpoint or adjust to 150/month (or founder time >10 hrs/week)
A: Month 3+ NPS survey with 100+ respondents validates First Round framework applicability to supplements
A: Month 6+ cohort data with 12-month LTV projection validates threshold or suggests adjustment

intelligence_gaps.gap: Meeting time budget (3.5% of work time)
intelligence_gaps.upgrade: B: Month 6 time tracking shows actual % of work time spent in rhythms (daily/weekly/monthly)
B: Month 3 reflection — did 30-45 min WBR catch issues solo reflection wouldn't?
C: Post-Q1 QBR reflection — did Monday WBR review prevent issues? B: Month 6 incident log (how many crises were caught early by loops?)
B: First crisis (ad account ban, stockout, churn spike) — did protocol work? A: 2+ crisis events show pattern (is circuit breaker rule effective?)
B: Month 6-9 cohort data validates threshold or suggests adjustment. A: Post-scale (Month 10+) — did gate prevent premature scaling issues?
UNRESOLVABLE at planning layer. HYP-006 Differentiation dependency. C: Month 6+ revenue trajectory shows if plateau is real. Mitigation: M28 market expansion if single-channel/ICP growth plateaus.
UNRESOLVABLE (individual psychology, not systematic). Mitigation: Day 180 PMF gate includes 'Founder capacity OK (not 80-hour weeks)'. If failing, hiring plan required before scale.
